things.name: Gravensteen Castle
things.description: Imposing twelfth-century moated castle of the Counts of Flanders in the heart of the medieval city

things.name: Ghent Altarpiece (St Bavo's Cathedral)
things.description: St Bavo's Cathedral houses the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb, the world's first great oil painting by Jan van Eyck

things.name: Graslei and Korenlei
things.description: Two facing medieval guild quays along the Leie river, forming the most picturesque streetscape in Ghent

things.name: STAM Ghent City Museum
things.description: Immersive museum charting 2000 years of Ghent's history set within a beautifully restored Cistercian abbey

things.name: Vrijdagmarkt
things.description: Historic Friday market square where medieval guilds assembled, dominated by the statue of Jacob van Artevelde

things.name: Design Museum Gent
things.description: Inspiring museum of applied arts and design from the Middle Ages to contemporary works in an 18th-century mansion

things.name: Patershol Quarter
things.description: Atmospheric medieval neighbourhood of cobbled lanes packed with authentic Flemish restaurants and hidden courtyards

things.name: Belfort Ghent
things.description: UNESCO-listed fourteenth-century belfry tower symbolising Ghent's civic independence, with sweeping city views

things.name: Ghent Canal Boat Tour
things.description: Guided boat trips weaving through Ghent's medieval waterways revealing hidden facades and centuries of city history

things.name: Werregarenstraat (Graffiti Alley)
things.description: Legal street art haven where internationally renowned artists continuously transform a narrow medieval alley
